rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical reductive cross-coupling of two vinyl bromides to form unsymmetrical 1,3-dienes using a Ni catalyst with sacrificial-like cathodic SET activation.
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction generates low-valent Ni species and/or reduces a Ni-vinyl intermediate to enable cross-coupling; vinyl bromide is activated via SET to give a vinyl radical that engages Ni.
electron_flow: At the cathode, Ni(II)/dtbbpy is reduced to a low-valent Ni that oxidatively adds/couples with one vinyl bromide; the second vinyl bromide is activated by SET to a vinyl radical which is captured by Ni, and reductive elimination delivers the 1,3-diene. TMSCl activates the SS anode/scavenges bromide.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a not detected (ND at 6.5 F/mol; trace at 13.0 F/mol); 1a recovered 51%/34% — reaction strongly inhibited, consistent with radical intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Little effect: 3a still formed in 62%; suggests the radical is not a free, BHT-trappable species (likely metal-bound or short-lived).

probe_reagents.name: (Z)-styryl bromide (1m)
probe_reagents.observation: Cross-coupling with 2a gave 3a (13%) and 3a' (Z-isomer, 14%) — partial loss of olefin geometry, consistent with a vinyl-radical intermediate that loses stereochemistry.

probe_reagents.name: nitrosobenzene
probe_reagents.observation: Used in one-pot tandem (procedure IX) to trap intermediate and give product 6i.

notes: TEMPO suppresses but BHT does not — authors interpret this as a Ni-bound/short-lived vinyl radical rather than a freely diffusing one. E/Z scrambling from (Z)-1m supports a vinyl radical intermediate. Pre-electrolysis and CV experiments referenced (sections 5.3 and 6) further support cathodic activation of the Ni catalyst before substrate engagement.
